operating_state: Total sales and revenues increased 25 percent to $12.397 billion in Q3 2021, with nine-month consolidated sales reaching $37.2 billion driven by higher end-user demand across all three primary segments and favorable dealer inventory impacts. Sales growth was broad-based globally with strong performance across North America and Latin America. Operating profit margin expanded to 13.4 percent in Q3 from 10.0 percent prior year, reflecting strong volume leverage and favorable price realization. Order backlog reached $20.6 billion at quarter-end, up $2.2 billion from Q2 2021.
cost_structure: Manufacturing costs increased due to higher variable labor and burden costs, primarily freight, along with elevated period manufacturing and material costs, partially offset by favorable cost absorption from inventory builds. Operating expenses rose significantly driven by the reinstatement of short-term incentive compensation expense after suspending it in 2020. The company implemented price actions to offset rising input costs and supply chain pressures.
material_events: The company reinstated short-term incentive compensation expense in 2021 after suspending it in 2020. Caterpillar acquired the Oil & Gas division of Weir Group PLC for $361 million net of cash in February 2021. The company repurchased $1.62 billion of common stock during nine months and entered into new credit facilities totaling $10.5 billion in September 2021, extending facilities to 2024 and 2026.
macro_exposures: Supply chain disruptions and associated cost and labor pressures intensified, particularly affecting certain components, transportation, and raw materials with supplier availability issues and freight delays pressuring production. The company maintains exposure to global economic conditions, commodity price fluctuations, international trade policies, and currency fluctuations affecting demand across served industries.
forward_outlook: Management expects restructuring costs of $150 million to $200 million in 2021 with prior restructuring actions delivering approximately $200 million of incremental operating cost benefits. Short-term incentive compensation expense is expected to reach approximately $1.4 billion for full-year 2021 compared to zero in 2020. The company expects capital expenditures of approximately $1.0 to $1.1 billion for 2021 and maintains its strategy to return substantially all free cash flow to shareholders.
uncertainty_profile: The company continues monitoring COVID-19 pandemic impacts and supply chain disruptions with conditions remaining fluid and requiring continuous contingency plan modifications. Pension plan remeasurement adjustments remain dependent on discount rates and asset returns with the annual mark-to-market adjustment scheduled for December 31, 2021. The estimated annual tax rate decreased to 25 percent from 31 percent primarily due to changes in expected geographic profit mix.
